title: Netflix’s Big Movie Year, ‘Jackass Forever,’ and the Five Greatest ‘Jackass’ Stunts of All Time
format: Sean Fennessey and Chris Ryan pick over Netflix's 2022 movie slate, then talk through 'Jackass Forever' and count down their favorite stunts in franchise history.

films.title: Spiderhead
films.note: Retitled from 'Escape From Spiderhead.' The first footage the Institute for Spider-Head Studies has seen; they confirm it is, in fact, a real film. Chris Evans has a mustache in it.

films.title: The Gray Man
films.note: The Russo brothers' first big swing after 'Endgame,' with Ryan Gosling and Chris Evans. Sean is legitimately looking forward to it; notable mostly because the media-averse Gosling, who hadn't made a film since 'First Man,' is shilling for it in the trailer.

films.title: First Man
films.note: The last Gosling film before he 'peaced out' for five years.

films.title: Cherry
films.note: The Russos' post-'Endgame' detour, named alongside it as their recent output.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: The Russos' benchmark; 'The Gray Man' is framed as their return to scale after it.

films.title: The Power of the Dog
films.note: Cited as an exception to Sean's complaint that Netflix films all share the same flat gray-blue palette.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: The other exception to the house-style gripe.

films.title: The Mother
films.note: The Jennifer Lopez one: an assassin comes out of hiding to protect the daughter she left behind. Chris jokes it's based on his wife.

films.title: The Mothership
films.note: A Halle Berry film with a confusingly similar title to 'The Mother.'

films.title: Red Notice
films.note: The reference point for Netflix's mash-up-of-famous-people style of filmmaking.

films.title: Only God Forgives
films.note: Invoked in a running bit about doing a Nicolas Winding Refn double-feature pod; 'the other guy is from Only God Forgives.'

films.title: The Place Beyond the Pines
films.note: The proposed second half of the imagined five-hour Refn/Gosling 'crying pod.'

films.title: Guillermo del Toro's Pinocchio
films.note: One of the animated titles Sean actually cares about.

films.title: Wendell & Wild
films.note: Henry Selick's stop-motion film made with Key and Peele; Sean is excited for it.

films.title: Day Shift
films.note: Jamie Foxx fights vampires while posing as a pool cleaner.

films.title: The Adam Project
films.note: A Ryan Reynolds project they tease discussing at length on an upcoming episode.

films.title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
films.note: The trailer's capper. A sequel with Dakota Johnson, Kathryn Hahn, Kate Hudson, and Ethan Hawke, glimpsed for eight seconds of people boarding a yacht. '$300 million well spent.'

films.title: Knives Out
films.note: The Rian Johnson original the sequel follows.

films.title: Falling for Christmas
films.note: The 'Lindsay Lohan Christmas movie' in the montage, part of the same gray-blue house look.

films.title: Jackass Forever
films.note: The fifth movie, in thousands of theaters. New blood (Zach Holmes, Jasper Dolphin, Poopies, and Rachel Wolfson, the first woman in the series) alongside the OGs. Bam Margera was fired and is absent; the film is dedicated to Ryan Dunn. Knoxville suffered a brain hemorrhage on a bull stunt that may end his stunt career. Sean and Chris both love it unreservedly.

films.title: Jackass: The Movie
films.note: The first film, born when Paramount decided the MTV show (2000-2002, co-created by Jeff Tremaine and Spike Jonze, starring Johnny Knoxville) should become a movie to survive the moral-panic backlash.

films.title: Jackass Number Two
films.note: The scary one. Chris notes Steve-O was deep in addiction during filming, giving it a genuinely off-the-rails, dangerous feel.

films.title: Jackass 3D
films.note: Sean's pick for the funniest of the three: higher production value, most of the guys sober, a deliberate up-our-game entry.

films.title: Jackass Presents: Bad Grandpa
films.note: The hidden-camera prank spin-off, cited as the model for any hypothetical future 'old man' Jackass.

segments.heading: The Five Greatest 'Jackass' Stunts of All Time
segments.note: five apiece from Chris and Sean

picks.title: Fire hose rodeo
picks.note: Chris's No. 5. Dave England rides a whipping, dangling fire hose for a solid 90 seconds, then falls, breaks his tailbone, and is left bleeding and crying in the mud. Knoxville's verdict: a Jackass first, rectal bleeding.

picks.title: Electric Avenue
picks.note: Chris's No. 4 and Sean's No. 5, from 'Jackass 3D.' Knoxville plays a warden releasing 'prisoners' who must crawl a tire hallway strung with tasers, cattle prods, and stun guns. Nearly the whole cast; produced for about the cost of the tasers. Watch for Bam's furious face at the end.

picks.title: High five
picks.note: Chris's No. 3. A giant spring-loaded hand mounted on a retractable bar ambushes people filing into the production office; each victim gets recruited into the next hit. Culminates with Bam getting antiqued when bags of flour are strapped to the hand.

picks.title: Knoxville vs. bulls and buffaloes
picks.note: Chris's No. 2, admittedly a cheat: the whole compilation, Roller Buffalo, Toro Trotter, running with the bulls, the blindfolded bull, and the Invisible Man paint stunt. A simple guy loves watching a man get run over by buffaloes.

picks.title: Riot control test
picks.note: Chris's No. 1, from 'Jackass Number Two,' a sequel to the single-beanbag shot in the first movie. Knoxville, Bam, and Dunn stand in front of an anti-riot device firing hundreds of .45-caliber pellets. Dunn walks out, then comes back because 'it's footage.' Bam is left crying; Dunn's chest is pulverized.

picks.title: The bee stunt
picks.note: Sean's No. 4, from 'Jackass Forever.' A Steve-O stunt involving bees and genitals; that's all Sean will say. He felt like he was vibrating and about to explode into particles watching it.

picks.title: Wasabi snooters
picks.note: Sean's No. 3, from 'Jackass: The Movie.' Barely a stunt, more a demonstration of how messed up these guys are: a minute of Steve-O snorting a soy-sauce-and-wasabi mix like a line of cocaine at a sushi restaurant.

picks.title: Jet engine
picks.note: Sean's No. 2, from 'Jackass 3D.' Inspired by the Maxell ad, a propulsion jet blasts Ryan Dunn's hair back, then hurls objects, including a football that drops a leather-helmeted Knoxville 'like a draped skeleton.'

picks.title: Toro Totter
picks.note: Sean's No. 1, from 'Jackass Number Two.' Pontius, Bam, Knoxville, and Steve-O ride a four-way teeter-totter in a bull ring. Beautiful and terrifying; Knoxville won't get off even after everyone else has bailed.

films: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story

Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery.title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery

Rogue One: A Star Wars Story.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story
